criteria.description: Provider package skeleton exists as purple-php/provider-bedrock with package-local autoload, dev autoload, tests, and Composer scripts.
criteria.evidence: WP01 approved at 65403b9. Evidence: packages/provider-bedrock/composer.json, phpunit.xml.dist, phpstan.neon.dist, .php-cs-fixer.dist.php, and README.md; composer validate --working-dir=packages/provider-bedrock --strict and composer install --working-dir=packages/provider-bedrock --no-interaction passed.
criteria.notes: Local monorepo path repository maps purple-php/sdk to the repository root.

criteria.description: Bedrock provider implementation and provider-specific tests moved out of root core into the provider package.
criteria.evidence: WP02 approved at 04c0901. Evidence: packages/provider-bedrock/src/BedrockProvider.php and packages/provider-bedrock/tests/BedrockProviderTest.php; former root src/Provider/Bedrock and tests/Provider/Bedrock paths are absent on the dependency lane.
criteria.notes: Namespace Purple\Provider\Bedrock is preserved under package-local PSR-4 autoload.

criteria.description: Core retains provider contracts, ProviderProfile::bedrock(), and Sdk::fromProvider() for optional provider composition.
criteria.evidence: WP03 approved at 69c9ee2. Evidence: src/Sdk.php keeps Sdk::fromProvider(), src/ProviderProfile.php keeps ProviderProfile::bedrock(), root composer check passed, and targeted PHPUnit for tests/SdkTest.php plus tests/ProviderProfileTest.php passed.

criteria.description: Core SDK runtime imports and convenience factory code no longer require the Bedrock provider package to be installed.
criteria.evidence: WP03 approved at 69c9ee2. Evidence: rg -n "BedrockProvider|Provider\\Bedrock|Sdk::bedrock" src tests composer.json packages/provider-bedrock returns only package-local references under packages/provider-bedrock.
criteria.notes: Root Sdk::bedrock() was removed; package adopters use BedrockSdk::create().

criteria.description: Provider package exposes an ergonomic Bedrock SDK factory/helper through core contracts.
criteria.evidence: WP02 approved at 04c0901. Evidence: packages/provider-bedrock/src/BedrockSdk.php creates a Purple\Sdk through Sdk::fromProvider(new BedrockProvider(...)); packages/provider-bedrock/tests/BedrockSdkTest.php proves the helper and rejects non-Bedrock profiles.
criteria.notes: The helper accepts profile, audit, policy, schema validator, injectable transport, and region.

criteria.description: Core composer check passes without root requiring purple-php/provider-bedrock.
criteria.evidence: WP03 review evidence plus WP04 lane-d rerun: root composer check passed with 113 tests and 437 assertions, PHPStan clean, php-cs-fixer dry-run clean; composer validate --strict passed; custom root composer.json dependency check confirmed purple-php/provider-bedrock is not in require or require-dev.
criteria.notes: Root validation still does not require AWS credentials, AWS SDK packages, sidecar services, native extensions, or live network access.

criteria.description: Bedrock package validation passes without AWS credentials, live network calls, or AWS SDK dependencies by using injectable transports.
criteria.evidence: WP02/WP03 review evidence plus WP04 lane-d rerun: composer check --working-dir=packages/provider-bedrock passed with 3 tests and 12 assertions, PHPStan clean, php-cs-fixer dry-run clean; tests use injectable transport fixtures in BedrockProviderTest and BedrockSdkTest.
criteria.notes: Package composer.json has no AWS SDK dependency.

criteria.description: Documentation explains the package split, installation shape, and migration path from root Sdk::bedrock() to the optional provider package.
criteria.evidence: WP04 docs update. Evidence: README.md adds Optional Bedrock Provider instructions and BedrockSdk::create() migration sample; docs/enterprise/README.md describes package-scoped validation and future AWS work boundaries; docs/architecture/001-enterprise-adapter-split.md records the implemented extraction.
criteria.notes: Docs avoid Packagist publication, separate repository, AWS SDK support, live AWS signing, and native/runtime extraction claims.

criteria.description: Roadmap and enterprise docs represent Bedrock as the first optional provider package split, not as a required core dependency.
criteria.evidence: WP04 docs update. Evidence: outline.md records Phase 5.2 Bedrock Provider Package Split as complete; README.md and docs/enterprise/README.md describe Bedrock as optional; ADR 001 marks Bedrock as the first implemented monorepo package split.
criteria.notes: Core SDK remains provider-neutral in roadmap language.

negative_invariants.description: Root composer.json must not require purple-php/provider-bedrock.
negative_invariants.verification_command: php -r '$json=json_decode(file_get_contents("composer.json"), true); foreach (["require","require-dev"] as $section) { if (isset($json[$section]["purple-php/provider-bedrock"])) { fwrite(STDERR, "found in $section\n"); exit(1); } } echo "purple-php/provider-bedrock not required by root composer.json\n";'
negative_invariants.evidence: Command exited 0: purple-php/provider-bedrock not required by root composer.json

negative_invariants.description: Root source and tests must not import the Bedrock provider implementation or call Sdk::bedrock().
Documentation must not claim Packagist publication, separate repository extraction, required AWS SDK support, required live AWS signing, or runtime/native package extraction.
negative_invariants.verification_command: rg -n "is published on Packagist|separate GitHub repository|requires AWS SDK|requires live AWS|ships as a native package|ships as a runtime package" README.md docs/enterprise/README.md docs/architecture/001-enterprise-adapter-split.md outline.md
